key_evidence: Sulyok refused to resign by the May 31 deadline and consulted the Venice Commission (Bloomberg, WashPost, Al Jazeera, Euronews, June 1)
Magyar confirmed June 1 the Tisza Party will launch a constitutional amendment using its two-thirds majority to enable removal of state leaders
Magyar specified the amendment is a GENERAL FRAMEWORK (not a person-specific tailor-made law) — a mitigant against the supermajority-override concern (Euronews, BBJ)

Pre-committed path from 2026-05-27 (Forward Signals): 'If Magyar invokes amendment and Sulyok removed: Sub-threshold INT +1.0 (executive-restoration positive offset to supermajority-override concern); document as boundary-watch' — this branch now fires
Methodology-novel category CONSTITUTIONAL-AMENDMENT-AS-EXECUTIVE-REMOVAL documented; general-framework design + Venice Commission consultation hold magnitude conservative

events.label: Sulyok boundary-watch resolved: constitutional amendment to enable presidential removal (general framework), pre-committed INT +1.0 fires

methodology_note: Boundary-watch RESOLVED per the 2026-05-27 pre-committed conditional logic. Branch fired: 'Magyar invokes amendment and Sulyok removed' path -> sub-threshold INT +1.0 (3.1->3.15 raw / +1.0 on 0-100 scale). Net-positive on contested valence: restores elected-government control over an Orban-era-installed head of state, pursued via constitutional process and framed as a general (non-person-specific) framework with Venice Commission consultation; offset against the concentration-of-power concern. Composite +0.2 (50.0->50.2), verified via canonical formula (integrationPremium 0). Sub-5pt magnitude; documented as a scored event for traceability because it is a pre-committed trigger firing on a methodology-novel governance category. No band crossing (remains Functional). CONSTITUTIONAL-AMENDMENT-AS-EXECUTIVE-REMOVAL noted as a methodology-novelty candidate not yet operationalized into a standing rule.
